title: Good traits — Jul 2025 self-inventory
html:
TL;DR

  • A short self-inventory written Jul 12 2025 — mid-Brotein-grind, pre-Austin-move. Yahir lists traits he believes he has (or wants to keep):
    • Reaching out and keeping in touch.
    • Unwavering passion for life.
    • Unconventional ambition.
    • Unconventional EDs (likely "educations" — auto-correct artifact).
    • Caring about people I truly care about.
    • Being able to take in weirdness and "andesy" things at face value and going in from there.
    • But letting small BS affect / ruin the day — the one negative he names.
    • Articulating life philosophy.

Key honesty

"But letting small BS affect ruin the day."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Negative trait listed alongside positives — the critical-self-evaluation is itself a virtue Yahir's been practicing since 2022 05 14 have trouble being honest about myself ("be honest about yourself").
  • "Unconventional ambition + unconventional Eds + caring about people I truly care about" — the Yahir character-thesis in three lines. Pairs with 2024 12 14 departing china's "Be so truly yourself it catches people off guard."
  • "Reaching out and keeping in touch" as a trait Yahir claims contradicts his self-criticism in 2024 10 31 dominic — "I regret not staying in touch the last few years when this developed." Possibly aspirational rather than always-true.

Open questions

  • "Unconventional Eds" — definitely educations, or is "Eds" a person?
  • "Andesy things" — Andean (related to South-American travel, peru / machu picchu / cusco)? Or something else?

Wikilinks
